dependencies: Certain components or modules of LC and MS instruments are manufactured by outside contractors in Singapore
Outsourcing of manufacturing of certain electronic components such as computers, monitors and circuit boards to outside vendors
A number of items are purchased from limited or single sources of supply
Consolidation among suppliers could result in other limited or sole-source suppliers
risks: Disruption of sources could have at least a temporary adverse effect on shipments and financial results
Price increases from suppliers including as a result of imposed tariffs could adversely affect margins
Prolonged inability to obtain certain materials or components could adversely affect financial condition or results of operations
Failure by outside contractors to perform obligations could adversely affect supply chain and financial results
If contractors experience significant disruption in services or institute significant price increase, the Company may have to seek alternative providers

regulatory_risks: Changes in governmental regulations and compliance failures could harm the Company's business
Regulation by FDA and similar foreign regulatory agencies
Regulations governing product design, development, labeling, manufacturing, promotion, sales and distribution
Significant political shifts may result in regulatory uncertainty and substantial changes in regulatory regimes
Policy, regulatory and enforcement changes introduced by new presidential administration and regulatory leadership in the United States may impact business and capital expenditure strategies
Failure to comply with applicable governmental regulations could result in product recalls, imposition of fines, restrictions on ability to conduct or expand operations or cessation of operations
Changes in laws or regulations associated with enhanced protection of sensitive types of personal information could greatly increase cost of compliance
Failure to comply with laws and regulations on privacy, data security or consumer protection could result in lost or restricted business, proceedings, actions or fines
Compliance with rules of industrial standards bodies such as International Standards Organization
Compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Administration regulations
Compliance with Sarbanes-Oxley regulations requiring adequate internal control over financial reporting
SEC rules under Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act requiring disclosure on conflict minerals
European Union and China Restrictions on use of certain Hazardous Substances in electrical and electronic equipment
European Union Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment directives
New or increased legal and regulatory requirements to reduce or mitigate impacts to the environment related to climate change
Changing customer and consumer preferences or increased regulatory requirements regarding plastics and packaging materials
Increased demands or requirements regarding single-use and non-recyclable plastic products and packaging
FCPA and UK Bribery Act compliance requirements

sanction_risks: Impact and costs of war, in particular as a result of ongoing conflicts between Russia and Ukraine and in the Middle East
Possibility of further escalation resulting in new geopolitical and regulatory instability
Effect of new or proposed tariff or trade regulations
Trade tensions and tariffs between U.S. and China and their impact on business
U.S. government has raised and proposed to further raise tariffs on several Chinese goods
China has retaliated with increased tariffs on U.S. goods which may increase cost of doing business in China
Changes in U.S. trade policy could trigger retaliatory actions by affected countries including China resulting in trade wars
Embargoes and sanctions as trade protection measures
